msgs.text:
I read the problem statement and prior investigations as an external proposal log. The prompt’s required format is the tame-format presentation with distinguished sigma,tau, implicit tau^sigma=tau^2, all later generators in the pro-2 core, and exponents represented modulo M=85667662080.The prior log’s only still-unrefuted candidate is the dynamic tame-inertia word; earlier scalar, plain-π2, and fixed-τ−2 variants have recorded finite-quotient failures.

The live candidate remains:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with π=π2∈Z and η=2π on odd tame inertia,r=xσ−πxσπx−1(xτ−η)π ((yτ)π)σy−1,where π≡40491355905,−π≡45176306175,−η≡50932630783(modM).The 2-primary coordinate of −η is irrelevant in this word, since that exponent is only applied to τ, whose finite tame images have odd order.

Audit of what is actually established The strong established package from prior turns is finite-tame consistency, not a proof of the absolute Galois presentation. The candidate has survived the recorded tests for:Ue,fab≃Z2ef+1⊕C2,and for the sign kernel,(kerκ)ab≃Z22ef+1⊕C4.It also has the recorded nondegenerate cup-product blocks and the correct dyadic sign-kernel behavior. What is not established is the formation/rigidity step: no prior turn has proved that the exact filtered (σ,τ)-operator action selected by the word is the arithmetic local Galois action over every finite tame subextension.The two stable equations should be kept in equation form to avoid the repeated reciprocal confusion in the prior log. For the sign-cover norm coefficient, with t the sign-cover involution and τ↦α=1 a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character,Da=j=0∑π−1(tαa)j is characterized by (1−tαa)Da=1−(tαa)π=1−t,henceDa=(1−t)(1−tαa)−1.

Thus Da is radical and −1+D−η is a unit on nontrivial odd tame-character sign-cover blocks.For the trivial tame-inertia block, with T the σ-cycle operator and s≡π(modf), the relation is(1+T−s)X+(1+T)Y=0.

Define Ps (T) by (1+T)Ps (T)=1+T−s.

Then Y=Ps (T)X+Yˉ, and h(T)=1+Ps (T)+Ps(T−1)has no nontrivial f-th-root zero because (1+ζ)h(ζ)=ζ−s+ζs+1=ζ−s(1+ζ2s+1),and 2s+1 is coprime to f.

New contribution: a single primitive-residual detector The latest prior turns organized the first marked IA obstruction into anti-cyclotomic exact-order components. I found a cleaner way to package the primitive exact-order test.

Let c be odd, and write a residual as

R(T)=j∈Z/cZ∑rjTj∈F2[Cc].

Define the primitive detectorΔc(T)=ℓ∣c∏(1+T c/ℓ)∈F2[Cc],where ℓ runs over the distinct prime divisors of c.

Then:the primitive exact-order c component of R vanishes⟺Δc (T)R(T)=0 in F2[Cc].

Proof: since c is odd, F2[Cc] is semisimple. On a character ζ of exact order d∣c, Δc acts by ℓ∣c∏(1+ζ c/ℓ).

If d<c, then for some prime ℓ∣c, d∣c/ℓ, so ζ c/ℓ=1, and the corresponding factor is 1+1=0. If d=c, then ζ c/ℓ=1 for every ℓ∣c, so all factors are nonzero. Thus Δc kills every proper-order component and is invertible on the exact-order c component.This is equivalent to Turn 71’s mixed top-digit tests, but it is more canonical: it avoids choosing a lower-inflation subtraction or a cyclotomic basis. It is also gauge-stable. Changing the normal-basis or Frobenius-lift gauge sends T↦Tu with u∈(Z/c)×, andΔc(Tu)=ℓ∣c∏(1+T uc/ℓ)has the same zero/nonzero action on exact-order components. So the condition Δc R=0 is invariant under all such unit relabelings.

Examples:

For c=9,Δ9=1+T3.

Thus primitive 9 vanishes iffrj=rj+3=rj+6 (j=0,1,2),which is exactly the block-constancy test.

For c=pq squarefree,Δpq=(1+Tp)(1+Tq),and Δpq

R=0 is the rectangle conditionru,v+ru′,v+ru,v′+ru′,v′=0 in Z/p×Z/q.

For c=99=9⋅11,Δ99=(1+T11)(1+T9),which gives the prime-power/squarefree mixed top-digit difference test without separately constructing a quotient basis.This should be the default diagnostic for future arithmetic residual computations. Given a normalized arithmetic-minus-candidate residual Rc (T), do not first choose a primitive basis; compute ΔcRc. A nonzero value is a genuine primitive first-IA obstruction. A zero value means the discrepancy descends to proper anti-cyclotomic order and must be tested at smaller c.

Updated first-IA test schedule For an anti-cyclotomic primitive order c, the minimal even unramified period is f=2n(c), where2n(c)≡−1(modc).The minimal tame degree for that primitive test is 2cn(c). The beginning of the unrestricted mathematical schedule is:c359111713331927254365n(c)1235465991076 f=2n(c)24610812101818201412 2cn(c)62054110136156330342486500602780 Thus the immediate arithmetic targets remain c=3,5,9,11, but the primitive detector gives a uniform way to decide each one:ΔcRc=0.The important point is that this is still not an arithmetic computation. It is a canonical reduction of what must be computed. No prior turn, and not this turn, has computed the arithmetic residual Rc even for c=3 in a proof-grade way.

Current candidate status I would keep the dynamic candidate as the only serious live submission candidate, but I would not mark it as proved. The candidate has strong finite-tame evidence, but the hard step remains:arithmetic filtered tame action=candidate filtered tame action over every finite tame subextension.At first IA depth, this equality reduces to the vanishing of normalized arithmetic-minus-candidate residuals in the anti-cyclotomic components. The new detector says exactly how to test the primitive part of each component without basis ambiguity.

ESTABLISHED: The dynamic candidate remains the only unrefuted candidate. I froze the stable equationsDa=(1−t)(1−tαa)−1,(1+T)Ps (T)=1+T−s.New this turn: for any odd c, the primitive exact-order residual is detected canonically byΔc(T)=ℓ∣c∏(1+T c/ℓ).

The primitive c-part of R∈F2[Cc] vanishes iff Δc R=0. This subsumes the block-constancy, rectangle, and mixed top-digit tests and is gauge-stable.

GAP: No arithmetic-minus-candidate residual Rc has been computed. The presentation is still not proved. The missing ingredient is either a dyadic formation theorem or explicit Kummer/Witt arithmetic proving ΔcRc=0 for every anti-cyclotomic primitive component, and then controlling higher filtered layers.

NEXT: Compute Rc for c=3,5,9,11 in a fixed Frobenius-lift and normal-basis gauge. For each, apply ΔcRc. If ΔcRc=0, descend to proper components; if nonzero, the dynamic candidate is refuted at first IA depth.[BREAKTHROUGH]
